request.question: CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will increase doxorubicin-induced cytotoxicity measured by LDH release

hypothesis.readiness_rationale: Core fields are present but comparator, threshold, and mechanistic rationale are not specifically defined; needs scientist review.
clarifying_questions: What is the comparator/control you will use for the experiment?
What is the numeric success threshold for cytotoxicity increase?
What is the mechanistic rationale for expecting increased cytotoxicity?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: CRISPR TP53 knockout iPSC cardiomyocytes doxorubicin LDH release
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will increase doxorubicin-induced cytotoxicity measured by LDH release

literature_qc.explanation: There are no papers retrieved that cover the same intervention (CRISPR knockout of TP53) in the biological system of iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes. The existing papers do not relate closely enough to this specific experiment type or context, as they either involve different interventions or biological systems altogether.

risks.mitigation: Pilot experiment recommended before committing full resources. Document all assumptions explicitly in the plan.

plan.objective: To determine the cytotoxicity in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes following CRISPR knockout of the TP53 gene.
plan.experimental_design: iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will be subjected to CRISPR-mediated knockout of the TP53 gene. The cytotoxic effects will be measured by lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) release after treatment. Comparisons will be made to control cells that have not undergone CRISPR intervention.
controls: iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes treated with 100 µM H2O2 for 24 h to induce cytotoxicity and promote LDH release.
iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes without CRISPR intervention under identical culture conditions.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 3 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Obtain iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes from a validated cryopreserved stock and thaw in a 37°C water bath for 2 min.
Expected outcome: iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes are successfully thawed.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Transfer thawed cells to a 15 mL tube containing 10 mL of pre-warmed Cardiomyocyte Culture Medium (CCM, e.g., RPMI 1640 supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin).
Expected outcome: Cells are in appropriate growth medium.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 8 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Centrifuge the cells at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to pellet the cells.
Expected outcome: Cell pellet forms at the bottom of the tube.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Remove supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of Cardiomyocyte Culture Medium at a density of 1 × 10^6 cells/mL.
Expected outcome: Cells are resuspended and homogeneous.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Plate 5 mL of resuspended cells into a 10 cm culture dish for expansion at 37°C, 5% CO2 for 48 h.
Expected outcome: Cells are evenly plated for culture.

DAY 1 | 60 min | CRISPR Knockout Design
Design and order two guide RNAs (gRNAs) targeting TP53 (e.g., 5'-GACCGCTTCAACCTGCCATC-3' and 5'-GGAACCTTCAGGCCACAGTG-3') from a verified supplier.
Expected outcome: Two gRNAs targeting TP53 are ordered.

DAY 1 | 30 min | CRISPR Knockout Preparation
Prepare a transfection mix by adding 1 µg of each gRNA, 1 µg of Cas9 plasmid, and 2 µL of Lipofectamine 2000 in 100 µL of Opti-MEM medium.
Expected outcome: Transfection mix is ready to use.

DAY 1 | 5 min | CRISPR Knockout Transfection
Add the transfection mix to the cells in a 10 cm dish, ensuring even distribution, and incubate for 48 h at 37°C, 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Transfection mix is evenly distributed over the cells.

DAY 3 | 10 min | Cell Recovery
Change the medium to remove transfection reagents by replacing it with fresh Cardiomyocyte Culture Medium and incubate for an additional 48 h at 37°C, 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Fresh medium is applied, and cells recover from transfection.

DAY 3 | 5 min | Genomic DNA Extraction
Harvest cells by trypsinization with 1 mL of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA for 5 min at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Cells are detached and ready for genomic DNA extraction.

DAY 3 | 10 min | Genomic DNA Extraction
Stop trypsin with 2 mL of Cardiomyocyte Culture Medium and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Cell pellet is formed post-centrifugation.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Genomic DNA Extraction
Resuspend the cell pellet in 500 µL of lysis buffer (e.g., 50 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.0, 100 mM NaCl, 10 mM EDTA, 0.5% SDS) and incubate for 1 h at 56°C.
Expected outcome: Cell lysis is complete, and genomic DNA is released.

DAY 3 | 20 min | Genomic DNA Cleanup
Add 500 µL of phenol-chloroform-isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) to the lysate, vortex vigorously for 10 sec, and centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 10 min at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Separation of aqueous and organic phases is achieved.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Genomic DNA Cleanup
Collect the upper aqueous phase and precipitate DNA by adding 2 volumes of 100% ethanol and 1/10 volume of 3 M sodium acetate, and incubate at -20°C for 1 h.
Expected outcome: DNA is precipitated from the solution.

DAY 3 | 20 min | DNA Quality Check
Centrifuge the DNA sample at 12,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C, discard supernatant, and resuspend in 50 µL of TE buffer.
Expected outcome: DNA is resuspended for quantification.

DAY 3 | 10 min | DNA Quantification
Measure the DNA concentration using a Nanodrop spectrophotometer (ThermoFisher) and ensure concentration is >100 ng/µL.
Expected outcome: DNA concentration is confirmed to be adequate.

DAY 3 | 30 min | LDH Assay Preparation
Prepare LDH assay kit reagents according to the manufacturer's guidelines, ensuring components are at room temperature prior to use (e.g., 1 mL of reagent mixture used per sample).
Expected outcome: LDH reagents are prepared and optimized for the assay.

DAY 3 | 15 min | LDH Release Measurement
Collect the supernatant from each treatment group of cardiomyocytes and transfer 100 µL to a 96-well plate for LDH measurement.
Expected outcome: Supernatant samples are prepared for LDH assay.

DAY 3 | 30 min | LDH Release Measurement
Add 100 µL LDH substrate solution to each well and incubate for 30 min at 37°C to develop color.
Expected outcome: Color development occurs in wells for LDH assay.

DAY 3 | 10 min | LDH Release Measurement
Measure the absorbance at 490 nm using a microplate reader and record the results for analysis.
Expected outcome: Absorbance readings are obtained to quantify LDH release.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Perform statistical analysis using GraphPad Prism 9 software to compare LDH release between knockout and control groups with a one-way ANOVA and post hoc Tukey's test.
Expected outcome: Statistical differences in LDH release between groups are determined.

assumptions: CRISPR-mediated knockout will effectively disrupt TP53 expression in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes.
Cytotoxicity will be proportionate to the level of LDH release measured from the cell culture supernatant.
iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes can be stably cultured and characterized to ensure consistent experimental conditions.
decision_criteria: Proceed if LDH release in knockout cells is significantly higher than positive control (p < 0.05).
Assess gene editing efficiency by Sanger sequencing; sequences should show insertion/deletion mutations in the TP53 target region.
Confirm TP53 knockout at the protein level by Western blot or ELISA. The expression should be significantly lower than control cells.
reproducibility_notes: Maintain consistent culture conditions, including medium, temperature, and CO2 levels throughout all experiments.
Use the same lot of Lipofectamine 2000 and reagents from the same batch to reduce variability.
Confirm the CRISPR components (gRNAs and Cas9 plasmid) for each run of experiments.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: not specified improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the cytotoxicity in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes following CRISPR knockout of the TP53 gene.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
